summary: The technical design addresses every business requirement R1-R13 with a concrete, traceable design element, and introduces nothing that no requirement asks for. Coverage: R1/R2 (system default + indeterminate->light) via the inline pre-paint init script (§4.2) reading matchMedia; R3 via the toggle partial rendered in both the sidebar and the sidebar-less auth layout (§4.4, D3); R4-R7 via the Stimulus theme_controller's synchronous class flip and aria-pressed/icon state sync (§4.3); R8/R9 via the persisted `theme` cookie taking precedence over system; R10/R11 via a matchMedia change listener gated on cookie absence; R12 via the CSS semantic-token layer plus the enumerated token sweep across ~23 views (§4.5, §5), which reconciles with the discovery notes' '~23 of 28 views' surface count; R13 via StatusHelper dark variants preserving the hue->meaning mapping (§4.6). No orphan/gold-plated design was found: the ThemeHelper SSR reader is a justified FOUC/no-JS robustness measure supporting R1/R2/R12 consistency, and the ui-style-guide update is a mandated project convention (CLAUDE.md), not scope creep. Advisory (non-blocking, correctness detail for the Implementer, not a coverage gap): toggle() writes the cookie but the design only removes the R10 matchMedia listener on next page load, so a system theme change within the same page session after a manual toggle could momentarily override the choice; removing the listener inside toggle() closes this narrow R11 window.
